name: Beowulf Full
description: Beowulf, a Geatish hero, is celebrated for his unparalleled strength, courage, and unwavering loyalty. Born the son of Ecgtheow, a renowned warrior, Beowulf's early life remains shrouded in legend, but his reputation precedes him. He journeys to the land of the Danes to aid King Hrothgar, whose mead hall, Heorot, is terrorized by the monstrous Grendel. Beowulf confronts and defeats Grendel in a fierce, hand-to-hand battle, tearing off the monster's arm. Seeking vengeance, Grendel's mother attacks Heorot, and Beowulf bravely descends into her underwater lair, slaying her with a giant's sword. Returning to Geatland, Beowulf eventually ascends to the throne and rules wisely for fifty years. In his old age, he faces his final challenge: a fire-breathing dragon guarding a hoard of treasure. Though he defeats the dragon, he is mortally wounded in the battle. Beowulf's death marks the end of an era, symbolizing the passing of heroic ideals and the transition to a new age. His story embodies the values of honor, bravery, and the importance of defending one's people against evil, solidifying his place as a legendary figure in Anglo-Saxon literature and a symbol of heroic virtue.
subtitle: The Geatish Hero of Legend

keyMyths.title: Beowulf and Grendel
keyMyths.description: Grendel terrorizes Heorot, King Hrothgar's mead hall, until Beowulf arrives. Beowulf confronts the monster unarmed and tears off his arm, mortally wounding him.

keyMyths.title: Beowulf and Grendel's Mother
keyMyths.description: Seeking revenge, Grendel's mother attacks Heorot. Beowulf dives into her lair and defeats her with a giant's sword he finds there.

keyMyths.title: Beowulf and the Dragon
keyMyths.description: As an old king, Beowulf battles a dragon guarding treasure. He defeats the dragon but suffers a fatal wound, dying a hero.

sources.author: Unknown
sources.description: An Old English epic poem, the primary source for the Beowulf legend.
sources.text: An Old English epic poem, the primary source for the Beowulf legend.

sources.author: Seamus Heaney
sources.description: A modern translation of Beowulf, making it accessible to contemporary readers.
